# WriteKit — MCP-powered publishing tool

> WriteKit lets you publish and manage pages and collections entirely through conversation with an AI assistant using the Model Context Protocol (MCP). No editor, no dashboard — just talk to your AI and it handles creating, editing, and publishing pages on your subdomain.

## What is WriteKit?

WriteKit is a hosted publishing platform where content is managed entirely via MCP tools. Each user gets a site at `{subdomain}.writekit.dev`. Pages are written in Markdown with rich extensions (syntax highlighting, callout blocks, D2 diagrams, media embeds). The AI assistant creates drafts, provides preview links, and publishes when ready.

## How it works

1. Connect your AI assistant (Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, etc.) to the WriteKit MCP server
2. Authenticate via browser on first use (GitHub, Google, Discord, or email magic link)
3. A site is automatically created at `{yourname}.writekit.dev`
4. Tell your AI what to write — it creates a draft and returns a preview link
5. The preview auto-reloads on every edit via SSE (Server-Sent Events)
6. Every edit is versioned (last 20 versions kept) — view previous versions with `?v=N` on the preview URL
7. When you're happy, tell the AI to publish — the page goes live

## MCP Server

- URL: `https://writekit.dev/mcp`
- Transport: Streamable HTTP
- Auth: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE (automatic browser flow on first use)

### Claude Code setup
```
claude mcp add --transport http writekit https://writekit.dev/mcp
```

### Cursor / Windsurf setup
Add to `.cursor/mcp.json` or Windsurf MCP config:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "writekit": {
      "url": "https://writekit.dev/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

### VS Code setup
Add to `.vscode/mcp.json`:
```json
{
  "servers": {
    "writekit": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://writekit.dev/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

## MCP Tools

### Pages

| Tool | Description | Required params | Optional params |
|------|-------------|-----------------|-----------------|
| `create_page` | Create a new draft page | title, content (markdown) | excerpt, tags[], slug, collection_id, position, tenant_id |
| `update_page` | Partial update of a page | id | title, content, excerpt, tags[], slug, collection_id, position, tenant_id |
| `delete_page` | Permanently delete a page | id | tenant_id |
| `publish_page` | Publish a draft (sets status=published) | id | tenant_id |
| `unpublish_page` | Revert to draft | id | tenant_id |
| `list_pages` | List pages with filters | — | status, tag, collection_id, limit, tenant_id |
| `get_page` | Get full page with markdown content | id OR slug | tenant_id |
| `append_to_page` | Append content to end of page (avoids re-sending full content) | id, content | tenant_id |
| `search_pages` | Full-text search (FTS5) | query | tenant_id |

### Collections

| Tool | Description | Required params | Optional params |
|------|-------------|-----------------|-----------------|
| `create_collection` | Create a page grouping | title | description, slug, sort_order (manual/date), tenant_id |
| `update_collection` | Partial update | id | title, description, slug, sort_order, tenant_id |
| `delete_collection` | Delete (pages become standalone) | id | tenant_id |
| `list_collections` | List with page counts | — | tenant_id |
| `get_collection` | Get details and pages | id OR slug | tenant_id |
| `reorder_pages` | Set page order in manual collection | collection_id, page_ids[] | tenant_id |

### Settings

| Tool | Description | Required params | Optional params |
|------|-------------|-----------------|-----------------|
| `get_settings` | Get site settings | — | tenant_id |
| `update_settings` | Update site title/description | — | title, description, code_theme, tenant_id |

## MCP Prompts

| Prompt | Description | Params |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| `write_page` | Generate a well-structured page | topic (required), audience (optional), style (optional) |

## MCP Resources

| URI | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `writekit://site/stats` | Page counts, collection counts |
| `writekit://site/settings` | Current site settings as JSON |
| `writekit://site/recent-pages` | Last 10 published pages |
| `writekit://site/drafts` | All draft pages |
| `writekit://site/collections` | All collections with page counts |

## Tenant resolution

If a user has one site, it's selected automatically. If multiple sites exist, pass `tenant_id` on each tool call.

## Content format

Pages use Markdown rendered with Goldmark and Chroma syntax highlighting.

### Supported Markdown features
- GitHub Flavored Markdown (GFM): tables, strikethrough, autolinks, task lists
- Headings (# through ######) with auto-generated anchor IDs
- Bold, italic, strikethrough, links, images
- Ordered/unordered lists, task lists
- Tables, horizontal rules, footnotes
- Raw HTML

### Syntax-highlighted code blocks
Fenced code blocks with language tags render with syntax highlighting, language icons, and copy buttons. 50+ languages supported including Go, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Rust, Java, Ruby, C, C++, PHP, Swift, Kotlin, SQL, Bash, YAML, JSON, and more.

### Callout blocks
Blockquotes with type markers render as styled alert boxes:
- `> [!NOTE]` — blue info box
- `> [!TIP]` — green tip box
- `> [!WARNING]` — yellow warning box
- `> [!DANGER]` — red danger box
- Also: `[!INFO]`, `[!IMPORTANT]`, `[!CAUTION]`

### Media embeds
Use `<embed src="url" />` for rich media:
- YouTube — thumbnail with play button, loads iframe on click
- Spotify — track, album, playlist, episode, or show
- SoundCloud — player embed
- Twitter/X — tweet embed
- GitHub Gists — loaded dynamically

### D2 diagrams
Use `d2` fenced code blocks for architecture and flow diagrams. Rendered server-side to SVG with dagre layout. Includes zoom controls and fullscreen viewer with pan/zoom. If D2 compilation fails, the error is surfaced as a warning in the MCP tool response so the AI can fix it.

## Site URLs

| URL pattern | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/` | Site index |
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/{slug}` | Standalone page |
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/{slug}.md` | Raw markdown of a page |
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/{collection}/{page}` | Page in collection |
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/{collection}/{page}.md` | Raw markdown of collection page |
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/search?q={query}` | Full-text search |
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/preview/{token}` | Preview (expiring link, 24h) |
| `https://{subdomain}.writekit.dev/preview/{token}?v=N` | Preview of specific version |

## Live preview

Preview URLs auto-reload when the page is updated via MCP tools. The browser connects via Server-Sent Events (SSE) and receives update notifications in real-time. No manual refresh needed — just keep the preview tab open while iterating with the AI.

## Page versioning

Every page update creates a version snapshot. The last 20 versions are kept per page. View previous versions by appending `?v=N` to a preview URL (e.g. `?v=1` for the first version, `?v=3` for the third).

## Pricing

- Free tier: full functionality on `{subdomain}.writekit.dev`
- Creator plan ($5/month): custom domain support

## Example prompts

- "Write a getting-started guide and add it to the docs collection"
- "Create a collection called 'Tutorials' with manual ordering"
- "Publish my getting-started page and move it to position 1"
- "Show me all my draft pages"
- "Update my auth page — add a warning callout about rate limits"
- "List all my collections"
- "Change my site title to 'Acme Docs'"
- "Search my pages for anything about authentication"

## Links

- Website: https://writekit.dev
- Documentation: https://writekit.dev/docs
- MCP Server: https://writekit.dev/mcp
